title: HIPAA Medical Records Release Authorization
description: A HIPAA-compliant authorization form allowing medical providers to release a patient's protected health information to a specified third party.
whatsCovered: Specific scope of medical records to be disclosed
Designated recipient and medical provider identities
Patient right of revocation and expiration details
whenToUse: Use this form when you need to authorize a clinic, hospital, or doctor to share your medical records or treatment history with another person or business.

fields.label: Purpose of Release
fields.placeholder: e.g. personal health evaluation, legal claim assessment

clauses.heading: AUTHORIZATION TO RELEASE PROTECTED HEALTH INFORMATION
clauses.body: Patient Name: {patient_name} | Date of Birth: {patient_dob}. This authorization form complies with the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) Privacy Rule.

clauses.heading: 1. Provider Authorization
clauses.body: I hereby authorize and direct {provider_name} to disclose and release my protected health information to {recipient_name}, located at {recipient_address}.

clauses.heading: 2. Scope of Protected Records
clauses.body: The information authorized for disclosure under this release includes: complete medical records, diagnostic test results, treatment logs, lab work records, billing history, and physician notes.

clauses.heading: 2.1 Inclusion of Psychotherapy Notes
clauses.body: This authorization explicitly includes the release of psychotherapy and counseling session notes, if any exist in the patient's records.

clauses.heading: 3. Purpose of Disclosure
clauses.body: This disclosure is requested for the following purpose: {release_purpose}. The recipient agrees to use this information solely for this stated purpose.

clauses.heading: 4. Expiration and Patient Rights
clauses.body: This authorization shall remain valid until {expiration_date}. I understand that I have the right to revoke this authorization in writing at any time by sending a notice to {provider_name}, except to the extent that action has already been taken in reliance on it.

clauses.heading: 5. Re-disclosure Warning & Governing Law
clauses.body: I understand that information disclosed pursuant to this authorization may be subject to re-disclosure by the recipient and may no longer be protected by federal privacy regulations. This form shall be interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Can I revoke this authorization after signing it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can submit a written revocation to your medical provider. Once received, they cannot release any further records (except those already sent).

faq.question: Does signing this affect my healthcare treatment?
faq.answer: No. Healthcare providers cannot condition your medical treatment, enrollment, or eligibility for benefits on whether you sign this authorization.
